architecture_fit.note: Exact GPU softmax attention with mean-max-weight as saturation score perfectly detects/quantifies the regime (AUROC 1.0), but it just recomputes the analytic reference rather than offering a distinct mechanism.

baseline_comparison.note: Attempt demonstrates no failing strawman of its own; it leans on the benchmark's linear/entropy oracle baselines (both ~1.0), which are upper bounds, not strawmen that fail.

faithfulness.note: Synthetic task with no model to ablate; method is exact so it is faithful by construction, but README never flags this or proposes a causal/ablation check as the rubric asks.

operating_range.note: Sweep spans logit_scale 0.1->100 (3 orders of magnitude) and the method tracks the reference exactly throughout; no breakdown shown since it is exact.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Entire model_fn is hand-written analytic attention reproducing the target behaviour, but the synthetic task makes this trivial — nothing is learned to contrast against.

visual_judgement.note: Demo plots silently fail: load_run reads a non-existent payload.json and falls back to empty sweep, so saturation panel and heatmap render as None; attn_weights are repr-strings that would break imshow anyway.

visualisation_rationale.note: README 'Why this visualisation' cleanly justifies the 2x2 sweep panel, the scale=10 heatmap, and the mechanistic max-weight emphasis tied to the goal — even though the described viz does not actually render.

notes: A correct, minimal, GPU-run implementation that computes exact softmax attention and uses mean-max-weight as the saturation score, scoring a perfect AUROC (1.0) and near-perfect entropy correlation. However it essentially recomputes the analytic reference, so it offers little novel mechanistic insight, shows no failing strawman of its own, and never addresses faithfulness/causal checks. The Demo viz is also effectively broken at runtime (loads a non-existent payload.json and falls back to empty plots), leaving the written rationale stronger than the actual visualisation.
